kicker: ET LEVEL I // MODES OF OPERATION

beats.narration: Last lesson you picked a coil arrangement. Now wire it. The mode of operation decides what your instrument can and cannot see.

payload.title: MODES OF OPERATION
payload.sub: Absolute, differential, hybrid — a wiring choice.

beats.narration: Arrangement is geometry — how the coil faces the part. Mode is wiring — how the coils are connected and referenced. The same bobbin body becomes either.
Run one scan, two channels. Absolute follows the gradual taper and the sharp pit. Differential cancels the taper to null, popping only at the pit.

payload.heading: Absolute versus differential
payload.note: ABSOLUTE FOLLOWS ALL · DIFFERENTIAL CANCELS COMMON CHANGE

beats.narration: That cancellation is power and trap. Differential rejects thermal drift and runs fast. But a flaw longer than the coil spacing shows only its ends.
Hybrids split the job — a driver excites, separate pickups sense. Learn what each mode hides, not just what it shows.

payload.sub: Know what each mode hides, not just what it shows.
